methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Furno Materials is a seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised $11M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 9 private frontier-tech peers and 1 public comparable (Lnza), representing the broadest set of climate and energy technology companies at similar or adjacent stages.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $11M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from seed to next stage, implies a valuation of $28M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A). The median valuation across this set is $150M, with a range of $110M to $200M.

Triangulation of these two methods yields a valuation range of $23M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $89M. The wide spread reflects the significant stage disparity between seed and Series A comparables and material uncertainty around Furno's technical progress relative to peers. The peer-median approach likely overstates value given Furno's earlier stage, while stage-progression may undervalue if the company exhibits differentiated technical risk profiles. Resolution of whether Furno's core platform demonstrates sufficient proof points relative to the Series A cohort will be the primary driver of valuation movement.
